text.title: ADR Template and Agent Gotchas
text.summary: ADR stub format for stack decisions and known failure modes when agents select languages and frameworks.

section.title: ADR Stub

rule.statement: Every stack selection for a project living longer than 6 months must produce an ADR with Status, Context, Decision, Alternatives Considered, and Consequences sections. Human sign-off required before implementation starts.
rule.rationale: Agents are bad at long-tail considerations (compliance, hiring market, vendor lock-in). The ADR forces explicit documentation so the decision can be revisited.

section.reference: ADR stub template to fill in

antipattern.description: Agent picks the stack mentioned most recently in the prompt, not the best fit for the requirements.
antipattern.reason: Recency bias in LLMs. Randomize the option order in the prompt and anchor the agent to read requirements first.

antipattern.description: Agent recommends last year's defaults (Create React App, webpack, black+isort) instead of current best-practice (Vite, Bun, ruff).
antipattern.reason: Training cutoffs lag. Anchor prompts with the current year and use this methodology as the authoritative current mapping.

antipattern.description: Agent copies format/test commands verbatim into projects with a different directory layout, causing path errors.
antipattern.reason: Commands assume src/ for Python and top-level for JS. Always adapt to the actual project structure before running.

antipattern.description: Agent applies this guide to ML, embedded, blockchain, or game domains.
antipattern.reason: The table covers web/API stacks only. Use domain-specific methodologies for other areas.

list.item: ruff — modern Python toolchain (format + lint): https://docs.astral.sh/ruff
biome — modern JS/TS toolchain (format + lint): https://biomejs.dev
cargo fmt + clippy — Rust format and lint: rust-lang.org
cookiecutter / degit — project scaffolders after stack selection
